mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit and construction activity, which amplifies aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and pricing power, which translates into immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, producing a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth provides a direct measure of aggregate economic expansion, and higher GDP readings consistently correlate with improved corporate profit expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows no predictive power for 60-day equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of months following CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which rapidly improves near-term earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 5-day equity returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized alongside industrial production acceleration, the economy enters an overheating phase that triggers supply constraints and inflation fears, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal predicts positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, invalidating the overheating thesis.
